claim_text: Primary HP13C applications include cancer and heart disease27,28,29,30,31,32,33 .

sub_claims.text: Nelson 2013 supports the set membership 'HP13C is applied to cancer' (it is an HP13C cancer application).

evidence.snippet: This first-in-man imaging study evaluated the safety and feasibility of hyperpolarized [1-13 C]pyruvate as an agent for noninvasively characterizing alterations in tumor metabolism for patients with prostate cancer.

The primary goal of this first-in-human study was to demonstrate the safety and feasibility of hyperpolarized [1- 13 C]pyruvate injections in men with prostate cancer. ... the secondary aims were to evaluate the kinetics of delivery to the prostate and assess differences in [1- 13 C]lactate/[1- 13 C]pyruvate for regions of cancer versus other tissues.
First-in-man metabolic imaging with hyperpolarized [1- 13 C]pyruvate was successfully applied in 31 patients with prostate cancer.

sub_claims.nuance: Nelson 2013 is the first-in-human HP13C pyruvate study in prostate cancer patients (n=31). It unambiguously supports the 'cancer' half of the manuscript's 'cancer and heart disease' set. Scope note: Nelson 2013 is specifically prostate cancer, not cancer broadly; the manuscript's aggregate sentence with 7 co-cites is appropriately backed by other refs for breadth. Nelson 2013 does NOT itself concern heart disease — its Discussion (L21) mentions cardiac disease only as one of several preclinical 'other disease models ... that show promise for translation' (refs 33, 35, 36), which is a forward-looking aside, not an HP13C heart-disease application in the paper.

remediation.suggested_edit: No edit required. Nelson 2013 is a correct cite for the cancer half of the 'cancer and heart disease' umbrella. The cardiac-applications half is carried by sibling refs in the co-cite bundle (malloy2011, morze2019, cunningham2016, etc.) and does not need Nelson 2013.

phrasings_tried: hyperpolarized 13C heart / cardiac application in Nelson 2013
attestation.indirect_attribution_check: Nelson 2013 is itself the primary HP13C prostate cancer trial; no indirect attribution concern for the cancer-application sub-claim.
attestation.out_of_context_check: The manuscript sentence enumerates an umbrella of HP13C applications. Nelson 2013 cleanly slots into the 'cancer' category; it is not being asked to carry the 'heart disease' half of the umbrella (sibling refs hurd2012, malloy2011, cunningham2016, morze2019, park2018 handle that side). The cite is in-context.
